---
title: "Zachary T. Rewolinski"
image: assets/profile.jpeg
subtitle: "Statistics PhD Candidate @ UC Berkeley"
about:
  id: hero-heading
  template: trestles
  image-shape: rectangle
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: zachrewolinski@berkeley.edu
      href: mailto:zachrewolinski@berkeley.edu
    - icon: github
      text: zachrewolinski
      href: https://github.com/zachrewolinski
    - text: "{{< ai google-scholar >}} Zachary T. Rewolinski"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=2t2Ojy4AAAAJ
    - icon: linkedin
      text: Zachary Rewolinski
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/zach-rewolinski/
---

::: {#hero-heading}

I am a fourth-year PhD candidate in the Department of Statistics at UC Berkeley, advised by [Bin Yu](https://binyu.stat.berkeley.edu/). Currently, I am interning at Microsoft Research, where I am working with [Chenglong Wang](https://chenglongwang.org/) and [Weijia Xu](https://weijia-xu.github.io/) in the Deep Learning Group.

My broad research interests aim to ensure that machine learning models are capable of making trustworthy real-world decisions in high-impact fields. Recently, I have been focused on a topic I refer to as *procedural data science*, which seeks to improve predictions and inferences by optimizing the underlying data science pipeline (i.e., the 'procedure' of doing data science). This includes analyzing the impacts of complex, often-overlooked interactions between early data preparation and modeling choices, and then using these insights to design systems where sequential choices compound into more reliable outputs. My research is supported by the [NSF Graduate Research Fellowship](https://www.nsfgrfp.org/).

I am also an Associate (part-time) Analyst with the San Diego Padres Research & Development team.

I graduated from Rice University in 2023 with a B.S. in Statistics, a B.S. in Computer Science, and a B.A. in Mathematics. While at Rice, I was fortunate to work with [Genevera Allen](https://genevera-allen.com/). 
:::

# Recent News

- **June 2026:** I started my internship at Microsoft Research, where I am working with Chenglong Wang and Weijia Xu in the Deep Learning Group.

- **May 2026:** I have passed my qualifying exam! Thank you to my chair, Avi Feller, as well as the rest of my committee: Amanda Coston, Deirdre Mulligan, and Bin Yu.

- **April 2026:** Our paper *Local MDI+: Local Feature Importances for Tree-Based Models* has been accepted to *Transactions on Machine Learning Research*!

- **April 2026:** A preprint for our paper [Sanity Checks for Agentic Data Science](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2604.11003) is available.

- **November 2025:** I have returned to the San Diego Padres R&D team as an Associate Analyst.

- **October 2025:** I have had the pleasure of presenting our work *Local MDI+: Local Feature Importances for Tree-Based Models* at Universität Regensburg in Germany.

- **October 2025:** Our paper *PCS Workflow for Veridical Data Science in the Age of AI* has been accepted to *Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A*!

- **June 2025:** A preprint for our paper [PCS Workflow for Veridical Data Science in the Age of AI](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.00835) is available.

- **June 2025:** A preprint for our paper [Local MDI+: Local Feature Importances for Tree-Based Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.08928) is available.

- **June 2025:** I started my internship with the San Diego Padres Research & Development team.
